title: AHA/ASA 2019 Guidelines for the Early Management of Patients with Acute Ischemic Stroke
source: American Heart Association (AHA) / American Stroke Association (ASA)
description: Comprehensive guidelines for hyperacute stroke evaluation, IV alteplase eligibility, mechanical thrombectomy, blood pressure management, and secondary prevention in acute ischemic stroke.
recommendations: TIME IS BRAIN: Door-to-needle time target <60 minutes; door-to-groin puncture target <90 minutes for thrombectomy
STROKE CODE ACTIVATION: Immediate CT head without contrast to exclude hemorrhage before any thrombolytic therapy
IV ALTEPLASE ELIGIBILITY: Administer within 4.5 hours of symptom onset (or last known well) if no contraindications; 0.9 mg/kg IV, max 90mg, 10% bolus then 60-minute infusion
ALTEPLASE CONTRAINDICATIONS: Active intracranial hemorrhage, major surgery within 14 days, BP >185/110 despite treatment, INR >1.7, platelet count <100,000, blood glucose <50 or >400 mg/dL
BLOOD PRESSURE MANAGEMENT: Lower BP to <185/110 before alteplase; after alteplase maintain <180/105 for 24 hours; for no-thrombolysis patients tolerate up to 220/120 for first 24 hours
MECHANICAL THROMBECTOMY: Indicated for large vessel occlusion (ICA, M1, M2 segments) within 24 hours of onset with favorable imaging profile (ASPECTS score >=6)
THROMBECTOMY IMAGING: CT angiography or MR angiography to confirm large vessel occlusion; CT perfusion or MRI DWI/FLAIR mismatch for extended window (6-24 hours)
ANTIPLATELET THERAPY: Aspirin 325mg within 24-48 hours of onset (delay 24 hours after alteplase); dual antiplatelet (aspirin + clopidogrel) for minor stroke or high-risk TIA for 21 days
ATRIAL FIBRILLATION: Anticoagulation for cardioembolic stroke — start 4-14 days after onset depending on infarct size and hemorrhagic transformation risk
STATIN THERAPY: High-intensity statin (atorvastatin 80mg) for all ischemic stroke regardless of baseline LDL
GLYCEMIC CONTROL: Treat hyperglycemia >180 mg/dL; avoid hypoglycemia; target glucose 140-180 mg/dL in acute phase
SWALLOWING SCREEN: Formal dysphagia screen before oral intake — aspiration precautions if impaired
TEMPERATURE: Treat fever (>37.5°C) aggressively — fever worsens penumbra; acetaminophen first-line
STROKE UNIT CARE: Admission to dedicated stroke unit reduces mortality and disability by 20-25% versus general medical ward
